symbols: light breaking through clouds — the glory of God's presence, divine revelation piercing through the veil of the natural world
vast canyon — the immeasurable depth and majesty of creation, evoking both the abyss of Genesis and the foundations of the earth
rainbow — God's covenant with Noah, the sign of mercy after judgment (Gen 9:13)
towering rock formations — the 'Rock' as a name for God, the enduring permanence of the Creator amid transient creation
storm clouds — divine power, theophany, and the awesome transcendence of the Almighty
description: A panoramic landscape depicts a vast canyon under a dramatic sky. Massive layered rock formations in warm reds, oranges, and ochres dominate the composition, carved into steep cliffs and terraced buttes. Light pours through breaking storm clouds at the center, illuminating the middle distance with a golden glow while the foreground remains in shadow. A faint rainbow arc appears on the far left. Rocky outcrops frame the scene on both sides. The canyon floor recedes into misty, atmospheric depth.

scripture_refs.reason: 'The heavens declare the glory of God; the skies proclaim the work of his hands' -- the painting embodies this psalm's vision of creation as divine self-revelation.
God's speech from the whirlwind: 'Where were you when I laid the earth's foundation?' -- the canyon's geological vastness evokes this rhetorical rebuke of human smallness before the Creator.
'I have set my rainbow in the clouds' -- the visible rainbow in the painting recalls God's covenant sign after the Flood.
The psalmist's hymn to God who 'set the earth on its foundations' and carved the valleys -- a direct poetic parallel to the geological grandeur depicted.
'God's invisible qualities -- his eternal power and divine nature -- have been clearly seen, being understood from what has been made' -- the theological premise of reading nature as revelation.
